'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Same mold as Maebashi Tenjinyama Mound<ref name="miyazaki" />. Extensively looted in the early Shōwa period<ref>{{Cite web|和書|url=http://www.miyazaki-archive.jp/d-museum/mch/details/view/1849|title=Cultural Properties of Miyazaki: Mochida Burial Mounds |accessdate=2018-10-23]]</ref>. |} [[Category:Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror forks]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[Category:Naka Hachiman Burial Mound]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Kofun in Japan]] [[Category:kkkkk]] [[Category:kkkk]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:Important cultural properties of Japan]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed kk]][[Category:Naka Hachiman Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed kk]][[Category:Naka Hachiman Burial Mound]] [[Category:refreshing to try to clear the links needed kk]][[Category:Naka Hachiman Burial Mound]] [[Category:kk]] {{Kofun navbar}}[[Category:kk]] [[Category:fixing the kk issue]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Naka Hachiman Burial Mound]][[Category:kk]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[Category:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Subst:MAINSPACE]] to [[Category:q-{{subst:NAMESPACE}}-q]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. revision.comment: Adding [[Category:前方後円墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:前方後円墳]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

revision.comment: linking [[Zenpokoenfun]]
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "-qq]]" to "-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]]"
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>

It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the [[Hasshaped Kofun|撥形]] (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period [[Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|筑前國続風土記拾遺]], mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
