revision.comment: adding kofun navbar
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=draft}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|12=draft}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|12=draft}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|12=draft}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|12=draft}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|12=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|12=draft}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|12=draft}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|12=draft}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|12=draft}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=draft}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|12=draft}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|12=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|12=draft}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}

'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=draft}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|12=draft}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}} with an expansive constricted section. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|12=draft}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|12=draft}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|12=draft}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.

The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.

Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=draft}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|12=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

revision.comment: 37 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:那珂八幡古墳 up to rev 101442545
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=那珂八幡古墳 |所在地=[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]那珂1丁目44 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|34|15|N|130|26|9|E|}} |形状=[[前方後円墳]] |規模=全長約75[[メートル|m]]、高さ6.5m |築造年代=3世紀中葉 |被葬者=[[福岡平野]]の首長（推定） |史跡指定=福岡市指定史跡 |出土品=[[三角縁神獣鏡]]・[[勾玉]]・[[土師器]]など |画像=[[ファイル:Nakahachiman Ancient Tomb.jpg|300px|那珂八幡古墳]] |画像キャプション=那珂八幡古墳
樹木の奥にあるのが古墳（右側の那珂八幡社の鳥居の奥に見える階段で上り下りする） |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Fukuoka Prefecture|relief=1|label=那珂八幡
古墳|lat_deg=33|lat_min=34|lat_sec=15.0|lon_deg=130|lon_min=26|lon_sec=09.0|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=28|float=center|border=none|position=top|width=200|caption=}}}} '''那珂八幡古墳'''（なかはちまんこふん）は、[[福岡県]][[福岡市]][[博多区]]那珂1丁目（旧町名：那珂大字宮の脇1丁目）に所在する、[[古墳時代]]発生期・出現期の[[前方後円墳]]である。[[複合遺跡]]・'''[[那珂遺跡群]]'''の1つ。福岡市指定[[史跡]]{{Cite web|和書|author=福岡市経済観光文化局文化財活用部文化財活用課|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=那珂八幡古墳|work=福岡市|accessdate=2023-02-15}}{{Refnest|group=注釈|「市指定」の史跡だが、福岡市サイト『福岡市の文化財』では、誤記で「市登録」と表示されている。}}。 == 調査 == [[1948年]]（[[昭和]]23年）に、神社境内で[[弥生時代]]の[[銅戈]]鋳型が発見された。その後、[[1971年]]（昭和46年）に[[九州大学]]考古学研究室が銅戈鋳型と同時期の遺構・遺物を発見すべく[[発掘調査]]したところ、神社がある独立丘陵が[[帆立貝形古墳]]であることが判明した。この調査は、[[那珂遺跡群]]発掘調査の第1次に位置付けられている{{Sfn|下村|1987|pp=1-6}}。 さらに、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）に[[福岡市教育委員会]]が市道那珂・竹下駅前線に伴う発掘調査を行なった結果、後世の改変をかなり受けているが、くびれ部が反るように広がる前方後円墳であることが判明した。また、後円部から見つかった2基の主体部のうち1基から、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]などの初期古墳に典型的な遺物が出土した。 == 位置 == [[福岡平野]]の中央に位置し、東の[[御笠川]]、西の[[那珂川 (九州)|那珂川]]に挟まれた平野上の丘陵にある。また、両河川は当時大きな入り江となっていたと推定されることから、当時は、入り江に半島状にせり出した丘陵の上に古墳が位置していたと考えられる。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は、長さ約75[[メートル]]、後円部の直径約48メートルを測る。墳丘そのものは、墳丘裾が[[鎌倉時代]]からの現代の住宅地造成、さらに[[太平洋戦争]]中の[[防空壕]]の設置によって削平されていたが、後円部は神社の社殿が立地することからも良好に残っていた。一方の前方部は、大部分が那珂保育園（調査当時、現在は福岡市教育委員会埋蔵文化財課那珂整理室が立地）によって、大部分が削平されている。発掘調査で見つかった墳丘から、正円形でない後円部やくびれ部の広がり方は、[[箸墓古墳]]と同じ[[撥]]形になる、発生期・出現期古墳の典型的な姿を示している。 [[江戸時代]]に編纂された『[[筑前國続風土記拾遺]]』に「三方に隍（カラボリ）あり」という記述がある点、昭和20年代までは、丘陵の周りが窪み状になっておりゴミ捨て場となっていたという記録から、周溝を有していたと考えられる。周溝からは鎌倉時代の[[土師器]]・[[青磁]]・[[石鍋]]・瓦が出土している。このほか、墳丘・周溝の下からは弥生時代後期の[[掘立柱建物]]・[[竪穴建物]]・[[井戸]]が検出され、墳丘の南側からは古墳時代後期から[[奈良時代]]にかけてのものと推定される[[甕棺墓]]が検出されている。 == 埋葬主体 == 主体部は後円部墳頂から2基が検出された。この内、墳頂中心部にあり、古墳造営当初の埋葬主体と考えられる1号主体部は大部分が社殿の真下にあるため、規模・副葬品は未だ不詳である。1号主体部の北側に位置した2号主体部も、一部が社殿の真下にあったが、長さ2.3メートルというさほど長大ではない[[割竹形木棺]]を石槨を造らず墳丘に直接埋葬した直葬形式をとっていることが確認されている。木棺内の土倉からは朱などが検出され、棺内から三角縁神獣鏡と[[勾玉]]・[[管玉]]・ガラス製小玉が出土し、棺掘り方からは赤色顔料が塗布された土師器の[[高坏]]が出土した。 出土した三角縁神獣鏡は直径約21.8[[センチメートル]]の三角縁五神四獣鏡で、同笵鏡が[[京都府]][[椿井大塚山古墳]]、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[湯迫車塚古墳]]から出土しており、また伝[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[富雄丸山古墳]]出土品やアメリカ[[フリーア美術館]]所蔵品が知られている。鏡面とチュウの部分に繊維が残っており、鏡面の繊維は平[[絹]]、チュウの繊維は[[荢麻]]であることが判明し、絹布に包まれた状態で副葬されたと考えられている。 == 被葬者 == 古墳の被葬者は、当時この地を支配した首長であり、両河川即ち[[福岡平野]]全体を支配した[[北部九州|北九州]]の中小首長であると考えられている。大和の発生期・出現期の前方後円墳と比べ規模は格段の差があるが、同じ時期のものと推定され、初期[[ヤマト政権]]との関係が注目される。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|first=洋一|last=井澤|title=那珂八幡古墳|year=1986|date=1986-03-31|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/17159|ncid=BN05690411|doi=10.24484/sitereports.17159|series=福岡市埋蔵文化財調査報告書141|publisher=福岡市教育委員会|ref=harv}} *井沢洋一「那珂八幡古墳と副葬の三角縁神獣鏡について」 『考古学雑誌』第72巻第1号 [[日本考古学会]] [[1986年]] *{{Cite book|和書|first=智|last=下村|title=那珂遺跡-那珂遺跡群第8次調査-|year=1987|date=1987-03-31|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/17171|ncid=BN02158511|doi=10.24484/sitereports.17171|series=福岡市埋蔵文化財調査報告書153|publisher=福岡市教育委員会|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == *[[那珂遺跡群]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[出現期古墳]] *[[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 那珂八幡古墳｜文化財情報検索｜福岡市の文化財] *[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html 福岡市の文化財一覧 - 考古資料] {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはちまんこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

さらに、[[1985年]]（昭和60年）に[[福岡市教育委員会]]が市道那珂・竹下駅前線に伴う発掘調査を行なった結果、後世の改変をかなり受けているが、くびれ部が反るように広がる前方後円墳であることが判明した。また、後円部から見つかった2基の主体部のうち1基から、[[三角縁神獣鏡]]などの初期古墳に典型的な遺物が出土した。 == 位置 == [[福岡平野]]の中央に位置し、東の[[御笠川]]、西の[[那珂川 (九州)|那珂川]]に挟まれた平野上の丘陵にある。また、両河川は当時大きな入り江となっていたと推定されることから、当時は、入り江に半島状にせり出した丘陵の上に古墳が位置していたと考えられる。 == 墳丘 == 墳丘は、長さ約75[[メートル]]、後円部の直径約48メートルを測る。墳丘そのものは、墳丘裾が[[鎌倉時代]]からの現代の住宅地造成、さらに[[太平洋戦争]]中の[[防空壕]]の設置によって削平されていたが、後円部は神社の社殿が立地することからも良好に残っていた。一方の前方部は、大部分が那珂保育園（調査当時、現在は福岡市教育委員会埋蔵文化財課那珂整理室が立地）によって、大部分が削平されている。発掘調査で見つかった墳丘から、正円形でない後円部やくびれ部の広がり方は、[[箸墓古墳]]と同じ[[撥]]形になる、発生期・出現期古墳の典型的な姿を示している。 [[江戸時代]]に編纂された『[[筑前國続風土記拾遺]]』に「三方に隍（カラボリ）あり」という記述がある点、昭和20年代までは、丘陵の周りが窪み状になっておりゴミ捨て場となっていたという記録から、周溝を有していたと考えられる。周溝からは鎌倉時代の[[土師器]]・[[青磁]]・[[石鍋]]・瓦が出土している。このほか、墳丘・周溝の下からは弥生時代後期の[[掘立柱建物]]・[[竪穴建物]]・[[井戸]]が検出され、墳丘の南側からは古墳時代後期から[[奈良時代]]にかけてのものと推定される[[甕棺墓]]が検出されている。 == 埋葬主体 == 主体部は後円部墳頂から2基が検出された。この内、墳頂中心部にあり、古墳造営当初の埋葬主体と考えられる1号主体部は大部分が社殿の真下にあるため、規模・副葬品は未だ不詳である。1号主体部の北側に位置した2号主体部も、一部が社殿の真下にあったが、長さ2.3メートルというさほど長大ではない[[割竹形木棺]]を石槨を造らず墳丘に直接埋葬した直葬形式をとっていることが確認されている。木棺内の土倉からは朱などが検出され、棺内から三角縁神獣鏡と[[勾玉]]・[[管玉]]・ガラス製小玉が出土し、棺掘り方からは赤色顔料が塗布された土師器の[[高坏]]が出土した。 出土した三角縁神獣鏡は直径約21.8[[センチメートル]]の三角縁五神四獣鏡で、同笵鏡が[[京都府]][[椿井大塚山古墳]]、[[岡山県]][[岡山市]][[湯迫車塚古墳]]から出土しており、また伝[[奈良県]][[奈良市]][[富雄丸山古墳]]出土品やアメリカ[[フリーア美術館]]所蔵品が知られている。鏡面とチュウの部分に繊維が残っており、鏡面の繊維は平[[絹]]、チュウの繊維は[[荢麻]]であることが判明し、絹布に包まれた状態で副葬されたと考えられている。 == 被葬者 == 古墳の被葬者は、当時この地を支配した首長であり、両河川即ち[[福岡平野]]全体を支配した[[北部九州|北九州]]の中小首長であると考えられている。大和の発生期・出現期の前方後円墳と比べ規模は格段の差があるが、同じ時期のものと推定され、初期[[ヤマト政権]]との関係が注目される。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group=注釈}} === 出典 === {{Reflist}} == 参考文献 == *{{Cite book|和書|first=洋一|last=井澤|title=那珂八幡古墳|year=1986|date=1986-03-31|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/17159|ncid=BN05690411|doi=10.24484/sitereports.17159|series=福岡市埋蔵文化財調査報告書141|publisher=福岡市教育委員会|ref=harv}} *井沢洋一「那珂八幡古墳と副葬の三角縁神獣鏡について」 『考古学雑誌』第72巻第1号 [[日本考古学会]] [[1986年]] *{{Cite book|和書|first=智|last=下村|title=那珂遺跡-那珂遺跡群第8次調査-|year=1987|date=1987-03-31|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/17171|ncid=BN02158511|doi=10.24484/sitereports.17171|series=福岡市埋蔵文化財調査報告書153|publisher=福岡市教育委員会|ref=harv}} == 関連項目 == *[[那珂遺跡群]] *[[日本の古墳一覧]] *[[出現期古墳]] *[[九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧]] == 外部リンク == *[http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 那珂八幡古墳｜文化財情報検索｜福岡市の文化財] *[https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html 福岡市の文化財一覧 - 考古資料] {{考古学}} {{日本の文化財}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:なかはちまんこふん}} [[Category:福岡県の古墳]] [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定史跡]] [[Category:前方後円墳]]

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|qq=draft}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|qq=draft}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|ja|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|13=User:Immanuelle/Zenpokoenfun|qq=}} with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the {{ill|Hashihaka Kofun|ja|箸墓古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hashihaka Kofun|qq=}}.
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}}, celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in {{ill|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|ja|椿井大塚山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|qq=}} (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including {{ill|Magatama|ja|勾玉|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Magatama|qq=}}, tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|13=User:Immanuelle/Haji pottery|qq=}} pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

[[sn:Naka Hachiman Kofun]] [[zh:三角縁神獣鏡が確認された主な古墳]] [[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] [[ja:福岡市]] [[ja:石塚山古墳]] [[ja:京都郡]] [[ja:苅田町]] [[ja:一貴山銚子塚古墳]] [[ja:糸島市]] [[ja:岩上祭祀遺構]] [[ja:宗像市]] [[ja:赤塚古墳]] [[ja:宇佐市]] [[ja:川部・高森古墳群]] [[ja:持田古墳群]] [[ja:児湯郡]] [[ja:高鍋町]] [[en:Naka Hachiman Kofun]] [[sn:aka Hachiman Kofun]]
<!--Naka Hachiman Kofun-->

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]
{{Kofun navbar}}

It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.

The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.

Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].

However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Historical mirrors in Japan
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Kofun in Fukuoka Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]
