<!--Naka Hachiman Kofun--> [[Category:博多区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Fukuoka prefecture]] [[Category:福岡県内の市町村指定史跡]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Municipal Historic Sites in Fukuoka Prefecture
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[Fukuoka City]], [[Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]
{{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]
{{Kofun navbar}}

Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].

'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>

The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. == Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.

Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].

revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
'''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

revision.comment: Bot: prepend Wikidata AFC comment via pages.txt
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642761]] Wikidata name: Naka Hachiman Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=Scallop Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nakahachiman Ancient Tomb.jpg|location={{ill|Naka|qid=Q65285160}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.570833333333|N|130.43583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}|part_of={{ill|Naka Sites|qid=Q11642794}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642761]] Wikidata name: Naka Hachiman Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. revision.comment: Bot: append |1= |comment= |WD= in {{ill}}
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nakahachiman Ancient Tomb.jpg|location={{ill|Naka|qid=Q65285160}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.570833333333|N|130.43583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718}}|part_of={{ill|Naka Sites|qid=Q11642794}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642761]] Wikidata name: Naka Hachiman Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Mikasa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489254}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=|1=Naka River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642765}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳|1=Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387347}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳|1=Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387347}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}

revision.comment: Bot: add wikidata link templates
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nakahachiman Ancient Tomb.jpg|location={{ill|Naka|qid=Q65285160}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.570833333333|N|130.43583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Naka Sites|qid=Q11642794}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642761]] Wikidata name: Naka Hachiman Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Mikasa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489254}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=|1=Naka River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642765}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳|1=Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387347}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}
== External Links == * [http://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553 Nakahachiman Kofun - Fukuoka City Cultural Properties Database] * [https://www.asahi-net.or.jp/~ri5t-mk/itiran/koukosiryo.html List of Archaeological Sites in Fukuoka City]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nakahachiman Ancient Tomb.jpg|location={{ill|Naka|WD=Q65285160}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.570833333333|N|130.43583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Naka Sites|WD=Q11642794}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11642761]] Wikidata name: Naka Hachiman Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|qq=|1=Mikasa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489254}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|qq=|1=Naka River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642765}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳|1=Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387347}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|qq=|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|qq=|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|qq=|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}

Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:那珂八幡古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site|image=File:Nakahachiman Ancient Tomb.jpg|location={{ill|Naka|WD=Q65285160}}|coordinates={{Coord|33.570833333333|N|130.43583333333|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|architectural_styles={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|WD=Q11268718|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}}|part_of={{ill|Naka Sites|WD=Q11642794}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11642761]] Wikidata name: Naka Hachiman Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Nakahachiman Kofun''' (那珂八幡古墳) is an early [[Kofun period]] [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] located in [[:en:Hakata-ku|Hakata-ku]], [[:en:Fukuoka City|Fukuoka City]], [[:en:Fukuoka Prefecture|Fukuoka Prefecture]]. It is part of the {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} and has been designated as a [[Fukuoka City Historic Site]].<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman>{{Cite web|author=Fukuoka City Economic, Tourism, and Cultural Bureau|url=https://bunkazai.city.fukuoka.lg.jp/sp/cultural_properties/detail/item_id:101553|title=Nakahachiman Kofun|work=Fukuoka City Cultural Properties|accessdate=2023-02-15}}</ref>
== Investigation == In [[:en:1948|1948]] (Shōwa 23), a [[:en:Yayoi period|Yayoi period]] bronze halberd mold was discovered on the shrine grounds. Subsequent excavations conducted by the [[:en:Kyushu University|Kyushu University]] Archaeology Laboratory in [[:en:1971|1971]] (Shōwa 46) revealed that the independent hill where the shrine is situated was a {{ill|Scallop-shaped Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|1=scallop-shaped kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop-shaped Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}, marking the first phase of the [[Naka Archaeological Site Group]] excavations.<ref>{{Cite book|last=Shimomura|first=Satoshi|title=Naka Archaeological Site - 8th Excavation Report|year=1987|series=Fukuoka City Archaeological Investigation Reports 153|publisher=Fukuoka City Board of Education}}</ref>
In [[:en:1985|1985]] (Shōwa 60), an excavation by the [[Fukuoka City Board of Education]] confirmed that while the mound had undergone significant modifications over time, it was a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] with an expansive constricted section. Two burial chambers were discovered in the rear circular section, one of which contained artifacts typical of early kofun burials, including a {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror.<ref name=FukuokaCity-Naka-hachiman/>
== Location == Nakahachiman Kofun is situated in the center of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], positioned between the {{ill|Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|ja|御笠川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikasa River (Fukuoka)|1=Mikasa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11489254}} to the east and the {{ill|Naka River (Kyushu)|ja|那珂川 (九州)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka River (Kyushu)|1=Naka River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642765}} to the west. It is believed that these rivers formed a large inlet in ancient times, with the kofun positioned on a peninsula-like elevation extending into the water.
== Mound == The mound measures approximately 75 meters in total length, with the circular rear section spanning 48 meters in diameter. Although partially leveled due to urbanization and wartime bunkers, the rear section remains intact due to the presence of a shrine. However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳|1=Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387347}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
== References == {{reflist}} == See Also == * {{ill|Naka Archaeological Site Group|ja|那珂遺跡群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Naka Archaeological Site Group|1=Naka Sites|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11642794}} * {{ill|List of Kofun in Japan|ja|日本の古墳一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kofun in Japan|1=list of kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11505809}} * {{ill|Early Kofun|ja|出現期古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Early Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|ja|九州・沖縄地方の史跡一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Historic Sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|1=List of historic sites in Kyushu and Okinawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11370155}}

However, most of the front section was removed during the construction of the former Naka Nursery School. The mound follows the {{ill|Hasshaped Kofun|ja|撥形|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hasshaped Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (paddle shape) design, characteristic of early kofun such as the [[:en:Hashihaka Kofun|Hashihaka Kofun]].
Historical records, including the Edo-period {{ill|Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja|筑前國続風土記拾遺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikuzen Kuni Zoku Fudoki Shūi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, mention surrounding moats, which were visible as depressions until the 20th century. Excavations of the moat area have yielded artifacts from the [[:en:Kamakura period|Kamakura period]], such as [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]], celadon ware, stone pots, and roof tiles.
== Burial Chamber == Two burial chambers were found in the rear section of the mound. The primary chamber, believed to belong to the original burial, remains largely unexplored due to its location beneath the shrine hall. The second chamber, located just north of the first, contained a wooden coffin directly buried without a stone casing. Analysis of the soil residues within the coffin revealed traces of vermillion, and grave goods included: * A {{ill|Sankakuen Shinjukyo|ja|三角縁神獣鏡|1=Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sankakuen Shinjukyo|13=User:Immanuelle/Triangular-Rimmed Divine Beast Mirror|qq=}} mirror (21.8 cm in diameter), similar to those found in [[:en:Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun|Tsubai Ōtsukayama Kofun]] (Kyoto Prefecture) and {{ill|Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja|湯迫車塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yusako Kurumazuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 備前車塚古墳|1=Bizen Kurumazuka Kofun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11387347}} (Okayama Prefecture). * Beads, including [[:en:Magatama|Magatama]], tube-shaped beads, and small glass beads. * A red-pigmented [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] pedestal dish.
Analysis of the mirror indicated that it had been wrapped in silk fabric before burial.
== Identity of the Deceased == The individual interred in Nakahachiman Kofun is believed to have been a regional ruler of the [[Fukuoka Plain]], possibly one of the smaller rulers of [[:en:Northern Kyushu|Northern Kyushu]]. Although smaller in scale compared to contemporary kofun in [[:en:Yamato Province|Yamato Province]], its similarity in burial goods and mound shape suggests connections with the early [[:en:Yamato state|Yamato state]].
